questions.directive: Read an email.
questions.prompt: What can be inferred about Dr. Johnson's participation in the symposium? It will primarily benefit her own research endeavors. It will lead to new commercial applications of solar

passage.text:
martinez.isres@dmail.com
From:
Subject:
Invitation to speak at ISRES
Dear Dr. Johnson,
As coordinator of the International Student Renewable Energy Symposium
(ISRES), I am pleased to invite you to be our keynote speaker at
Lisbon University on October 25.
Every year, ISRES brings students together from around the world to
explore recent advances in renewable energy, facilitating the exchange of
groundbreaking ideas via panel discussions, workshops, and networking
sessions.
Given your reputation among solar technology researchers, your
participation would enrich our conference immensely. This year's focus is
photovoltaics.
We kindly request confirmation of your availability by September 15 to
enable us to finalize the agenda and coordinate your travel itinerary. Your
accommodation will be provided at the four-star Lisbon Grand Hotel,
conveniently located downtown near the symposium venue and many
restaurants.
We are looking forward to hearing from you. Should you have any
questions, please contact me directly via email.
Warm regards,
Paul Martinez

It will require organizers to find a larger conference room for the keynote address.
It will enhance the prestige and quality of the symposium.
I

questions.directive: Read the passage and choose the best answer.
questions.prompt: The word "respite" in the passage is closest in meaning to break

passage.text:
Urban green spaces, such as parks and community gardens, enhance city life for residents. The contrast between green spaces and the surrounding concrete and glass structures can be a visual respite; such spaces also provide residents with a place to relax and connect with nature, which can significantly improve mental health, reduce stress, and encourage physical activity.

Urban green spaces also contribute to environmental sustainability. They provide a cooler spot within the concrete heat traps surrounding them, absorb rainwater and reduce flooding, and increase biodiversity by providing habitats for various species.

Recent initiatives have focused on creating more green spaces in underserved areas. Some cities are converting abandoned lots into community gardens or small parks. These projects not only provide green spaces but also involve community members in the planning and maintenance of them, fostering a sense of ownership and responsibility.

While urban green spaces offer a host of benefits, they are not without drawbacks, When parks and gardens are added in city neighborhoods, they often increase the value of nearby properties. This can unintentionally lead to the displacement of longtime residents who can no longer afford rising rents or property taxes. Another challenge is preserving these spaces-it can be difficult to secure the funding necessary to maintain them.

options.text: addition
symbol
surprise
I

questions.directive: Read the passage and choose the best answer.
questions.prompt: Which of the following best summarizes the main point of the passage?

passage.text:
Stars form in the relatively dense, egg-shaped cores of molecular clouds made of dust and gases. Scientists mapping these dense cores must use radio waves to penetrate the dust and gases that prevent light from escaping molecular clouds; satellite images of these regions show nothing but large, black areas of space.

Recent observations of the Perseus molecular cloud may have surprising implications for previously accepted conclusions about the Sun. A radio wave sweep of the Perseus cloud's dense cores revealed that the youngest stars existing in the cloud all occurred in pairs, or binary twins, orbiting a common point. Stars of older binary systems, of which there were fewer, appeared closer together than stars of younger binary systems.

Scientists used current knowledge about stellar growth, decay, and destruction to predict the paths along which binary stars traveled throughout the molecular cloud. Their estimates would align with current understandings only if all stars approximately the same size as the Sun had begun as members of a binary system that first moved closer to each other and later separated. This rule would also apply to the Sun, whose twin likely separated following approximately one million years of coexistence.

options.text: Binary stars alternate between separating and getting closer over time.
Star development takes place in the dense cores of molecular clouds.
The Sun formed in the same way that the Perseus molecular cloud did.
Stars the size of the Sun begin their development with a binary twin.

passage.text:
Landscape connectivity refers to how easily organisms can move across a landscape to access resources, find mates, disperse seeds, or migrate. This concept is crucial in landscape ecology, as in all cases o

it significantly impacts the survival and movement of wildlife. Fragmentation of habitats due to human activities, such as urbanization and agriculture, can reduce connectivity, leading to isolated populations and, in turn, decreased biodiversity.

One approach to enhancing landscape connectivity is through the creation of wildlife corridors. These corridors are strips of natural habitat that connect isolated patches, allowing animals to move more freely. For example, bridges and underpasses designed specifically for wildlife can mitigate the effects of roads and highways.

Recent studies have shown that landscape connectivity benefits wildlife and has positive effects on ecosystem services. Improved connectivity can enhance pollination, seed dispersal, and pest control, which are vital for healthy ecosystems. Additionally, connecting habitats can help species adapt to climate change by providing migration pathways.

However, creating wildlife corridors is not without challenges. It requires careful planning and consideration of various factors, such as the specific needs of target species, the existing landscape features, and potential human-wildlife conflicts. Despite these challenges, efforts to improve landscape connectivity are crucial for the conservation of biodiversity and

as a later consequence
unfortunately
before that
